name: ExtensionSubscription_Status
title: R5: requested | active | error | off | entered-in-error
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Subscription.status` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Subscription.status` 1..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Subscription.status` 1..1 `code`
*  R4B: `Subscription.status` 1..1 `code`
*  R4: `Subscription.status` 1..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Subscription.status` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Subscription.status` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The target context `Subscription.status` is a modifier element, so this extension does not need to be defined as a modifier.

element.short: R5: requested | active | error | off | entered-in-error
element.comment:
Element `Subscription.status` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Subscription.status` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The target context `Subscription.status` is a modifier element, so this extension does not need to be defined as a modifier.
A client can only submit subscription resources in the requested or off state. Only the server can  move a subscription from requested to active, and then to error. Either the server or the client can turn a subscription off.

This element is labeled as a modifier because the status contains codes that mark the resource as not currently valid.

element.definition: The status of the subscription, which marks the server state for managing the subscription.
element.comment:
A client can only submit subscription resources in the requested or off state. Only the server can  move a subscription from requested to active, and then to error. Either the server or the client can turn a subscription off.

This element is labeled as a modifier because the status contains codes that mark the resource as not currently valid.
